data.title: Dow Industrials Hit First Intraday High of 2025, Powered by UnitedHealth Shares — WSJ
data.description:
By Vicky Ge Huang

A major rally in UnitedHealth Group stock propelled the Dow Jones Industrial Average to its first intraday record of 2025.

UnitedHealth, the Dow's biggest laggard since early April, jumped 12% on Friday after a filing showed that Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway took a roughly $1.6 billion position in the healthcare company during the second quarter. UnitedHealth stock remains down 40% for the year.

Friday's gains faded in afternoon trading, leaving the Dow Industrials just short of its closing record. Nevertheless, investors view its climb as a sign of the rally's expanding breadth and depth, considering that a handful of megacap tech stocks, known as the Magnificent Seven, have driven most of the S&P 500's returns in recent years.

"There's a lot of market optimism and I am happy to see the Dow moving," said David Bahnsen, chief investment officer of The Bahnsen Group. "Because if all you have is the S&P and Nasdaq making a new high, it probably means that we're just getting more and more top-heavy with those tech names. But when the Dow is doing it, that's not the explanation."

The Dow climbed 35 points, or just shy of 0.1%, after earlier touching 45203.52, an all-time high. The S&P 500 fell 0.3%, while the tech-focused Nasdaq composite lost 0.4%.

The Dow last closed at an all-time high in December. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq clinched their 18th and 20th record closes of 2025 this week, respectively.

The delay is a consequence of the Dow's price-weighted methodology, which gives its high-price underperformers this year, including UnitedHealth and Salesforce, a disproportionately large impact on its performance.

The Dow's portfolio of 30 stocks is also heavily weighted toward industrial and financial companies such as Boeing and Goldman Sachs, giving it less exposure to technology and other high-growth companies that have driven the broader market rally this year. Goldman has been the benchmark's biggest contributor to this year's gains, rising 28%.

All three major indexes ended the week higher. The Dow led the gains with a 1.7% increase. The S&P 500 advanced 0.9%, and the Nasdaq added 0.8%.

The yield on the 10-year Treasury note, a key indicator of borrowing costs across the economy, inched higher to 4.325%. Yields rise as prices fall.

On Friday, the Commerce Department said retail sales ticked up 0.5% in July from June, suggesting that consumer spending remains resilient despite trade and economic uncertainty. However, the latest readout from the University of Michigan's long-running sentiment survey shows that consumers' mood has soured due to expectations of more inflation ahead.

Earlier this week, a mild government report on consumer prices boosted hopes that the Federal Reserve would cut interest rates in September. But on Thursday, hotter-than-expected wholesale inflation data renewed doubts about the rate outlook.

Interest-rate futures signaled by Friday afternoon that traders see a September cut as an 87% likelihood, down from 92% on Thursday, according to CME Group data.

"Economic growth is moderating and that should continue to support the idea that we're moving past the need for restrictive policy," said Trevor Slaven, global head of asset allocation at Barings. "I do expect that the Fed is going to cut at least twice this year."

Chip stocks declined after President Trump said he could announce tariffs on semiconductor imports next week, indicating that the tariffs would start at lower levels and then rise over time.

Applied Materials plummeted 14% after the semiconductor-equipment maker forecast lower profit and revenue this quarter. Rival KLA and Lam Research also fell 8.4% and 7.3%, respectively.

Shares of Intel gained 2.9% after reports that the chip maker and the Trump administration are discussing the government potentially taking a stake in the company.

Overseas, Hong Kong's benchmark dropped about 1% after data showed China's economy slowed in July. The Stoxx Europe 600 inched lower by less than 0.1% ahead of President Trump's face-to-face meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin to discuss prospects for ending the Ukraine war.

Write to Vicky Ge Huang at vicky.huang@wsj.com

data.title: BRINKER INTERNATIONAL, INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Brinker International, Inc., the parent company of popular restaurant brands Chili’s Grill & Bar and Maggiano’s Little Italy, has released its annual 10-K report for the fiscal year 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth, strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing financial flexibility, and the challenges the company faces in the competitive restaurant industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $5,384.2 million, reflecting a 21.9% increase from the previous fiscal year, driven by higher traffic, favorable menu item mix, and menu pricing.
  • Operating Income: $512.0 million, representing a 9.5% margin of Total Revenues, up from 5.2% in the prior year, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $383.1 million, a significant increase from $155.3 million in the previous year, attributed to higher revenues and effective cost management.
  • Basic Net Income Per Share: $8.60, compared to $3.49 in the prior year, reflecting the company's strong financial performance.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Share: $8.32, up from $3.40 in the previous year, highlighting the company's improved profitability.

Business Highlights

  • Restaurant Brands: Brinker International, Inc. operates two main restaurant brands: Chili’s Grill & Bar and Maggiano’s Little Italy. Chili’s is a leader in the casual dining industry with a global presence, while Maggiano’s offers Italian-American cuisine with a focus on special occasions and large parties.
  • Geographical Performance: Chili’s has a global presence with restaurants in the United States, 27 other countries, and two U.S. territories. The international expansion is driven by development agreements with franchise partners, with 30 new international locations opened in fiscal 2025.
  • Sales Units: In fiscal 2025, the average annual net sales per Company-owned Chili’s restaurant was $4.5 million, and the average revenue per meal was approximately $21.90 per guest. For Maggiano’s, the average annual sales per restaurant was $9.9 million, with an average revenue per meal of approximately $39.06 per guest.
  • New Restaurant Openings: During fiscal 2025, Brinker opened new Company-owned restaurants in strategically desirable markets, focusing on both major metropolitan areas and smaller market areas. The company plans to open 5 new Chili’s domestic locations in fiscal 2026.
  • Franchise Development: In fiscal 2025, 34 franchise-operated restaurants were opened, including 3 domestic and 30 international Chili’s locations. The company projects 26-32 new franchise openings in fiscal 2026.
  • Operational Strategy: Chili’s strategy focuses on simplifying the menu to core items like burgers and fajitas, enhancing digital experiences, and improving hospitality. Maggiano’s emphasizes warm hospitality and catering to special occasions, with a focus on banquet facilities and carry-out options.
  • Future Outlook: Brinker plans to continue expanding its restaurant brands domestically and internationally, with a focus on markets that improve competitive position and profitability. The company also aims to enhance digital and off-premise dining experiences to meet evolving guest preferences.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Financial Flexibility: Brinker International, Inc. has focused on enhancing its financial flexibility by amending its revolving credit facility, increasing the capacity from $900 million to $1 billion, which matures on May 1, 2030. This strategic move is aimed at supporting the company's liquidity and capital expenditure needs.
  • Debt Management: The company has been actively managing its debt profile by repaying $350 million of 5.00% senior notes using borrowings under the revolving credit facility.
  • Capital Management: The company has been proactive in capital management through its share repurchase program, repurchasing 1.0 million shares for $76 million in fiscal 2025. The Board of Directors authorized an additional $400 million under the share repurchase program, increasing the total available authority to $507 million. No dividends were declared in fiscal 2025 or 2024, reflecting a focus on share repurchases and debt management.
  • Future Outlook: Brinker International plans to maintain a strong focus on cash flow generation and financial flexibility to navigate the unpredictable macroeconomic environment, including commodity and labor inflation and supply chain disruptions. The company believes its current cash position, coupled with cash generated from operations and the available credit facility, will be sufficient to meet its capital expenditure and working capital needs for at least the next twelve months. The company will continue to assess the macro environment and adjust its capital allocation strategy, including share repurchases, based on market conditions and trends.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: Brinker International faces challenges in maintaining brand consistency and managing franchise operations, especially in international markets where cultural and regulatory differences may impact product specifications and operations.
  • Strategic and Operational Risks: The company faces risks including the inability to successfully design and execute business strategies, changes in consumer preferences, and food safety incidents. The reliance on third-party delivery providers poses risks related to service quality and availability. Additionally, the company faces challenges in recruiting and retaining high-quality management and team members due to a competitive labor market.
  • Macroeconomic and Industry Risks: Economic conditions, such as inflation and fluctuations in energy costs, can negatively impact consumer discretionary spending and the company’s financial performance. Supply chain disruptions and shortages in food and other products may increase costs or reduce revenues.
  • Information and Technology-Related Risks: Cybersecurity threats and the protection of confidential information are critical concerns. The company’s technology systems are essential for operations, and any failure or breach could significantly impact reputation and financial condition.
  • Financial Risks: Potential downgrades in credit ratings could impact the company’s ability to access capital. Changes in the market price of common stock or impairment of goodwill could adversely affect financial position and results of operations.
  • Legal and Regulatory Risks: Compliance with various federal, state, and local laws and regulations, including employment and labor laws, is essential. Changes in tax laws and unanticipated tax liabilities could also adversely affect profitability.
  • Management Strategies: The company’s operating results have been impacted by geopolitical and macroeconomic events, leading to inflation in wages and food costs. Management is focused on strategies to grow sales, increase profits, and engage team members. The company is investing in technology and off-premise options to enhance the guest experience and drive sales.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in commodity prices and interest rates. These risks could impact the cost of operations and financial results. The company’s ability to manage these risks is critical to maintaining profitability and competitive advantage.

SEC Filing: BRINKER INTERNATIONAL, INC [ EAT ] - 10-K - Aug. 15, 2025

data.title: World equities flat, crude oil prices fall as Trump, Putin hold high-stakes talks
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dow edges higher after hitting record intra-day high
  • Benchmark S&P 500 and Nasdaq fall
  • Crude oil prices shed $1 as Trump-Putin meeting begins
  • US dollar weakens, gold prices rise
  • US Treasury bond prices rises across board

Global stocks were flat but still traded near record highs on Friday as U.S. President Donald Trump and his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin held high-stakes talks in Alaska over Ukraine.

U.S. Treasury bond prices fell across the board with markets anticipating a Federal Reserve interest rate cut.

The Dow hit an intra-day record high during the session, becoming the last of Wall Street's main indexes to climb to a new peak this week. The benchmark S&P 500 and the Nasdaq dropped, dragged down mainly by technology, financials, industrials and utilities stocks. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.08%, the S&P 500 fell 0.29% and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.40%.

"This market continues to move higher and the story is just earnings and margins," said Talley Leger, chief market strategist at The Wealth Consulting Group in New Jersey.

"The inflation numbers that we saw this week were mostly services and in a services-based economy like ours, this is good for profit margins."

Data showed that U.S. retail sales increased solidly in July, rising 0.5% from the prior month, after an unexpected spike in producer price data on Thursday renewed inflation concerns and pared market expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts this year.

European shares touched a near five-month high before pulling back, as investors drew encouragement from a largely positive earnings season. The pan-European STOXX 600 index finished flat at 0.06%.

The MSCI All Country World Index EURONEXT:IACWI consolidated recent gains. It was last flat at 951.70, just shy of the record level of 954.21 set on Wednesday.

Trump and Putin met face to face in Alaska in a high-stakes meeting that could determine whether a ceasefire can be reached in the deadliest war in Europe since World War Two.

Trump has said a second summit involving Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy could follow if the talks with Putin go well.

Details and the longevity of any agreement will be key, and for now investors are on standby. Ukraine's government bonds - key indicators of the mood - have largely stalled in recent days at a still-distressed 55 cents on the dollar.

"There's still a small degree of risk premium in European markets because of the war. Any type of resolution will ultimately pare that back," said Shaniel Ramjee, co-head of multi-asset at Pictet Asset Management, adding that oil and other commodity prices could also react.

"But I think that the market has learnt not to expect too much from these negotiations. Ultimately, Zelenskiy and the Europeans are not invited. They will need to be involved in any final negotiation," Ramjee added.

The two-year note (US2YT=RR) yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Fed, rose 1.1 basis points to 3.751%, paring earlier losses. The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes rose 2.7 basis points to 4.32%.

In currency markets, the dollar weakened 0.38% to 146.72 against the Japanese yen and was down 0.15% at 0.806 against the Swiss franc . The euro > was up 0.48% at $1.1702.

The dollar index , which tracks the greenback against a basket of six major currencies, was last trading down 0.34% at 97.85.

Japanese GDP data released on Friday showed the economy expanding by an annualised 1.0% in the April-to-June quarter, beating analyst estimates.

Brent crude fell 1.5% to settle at $66.85 per barrel. U.S. crude fell 1.8% to settle at $62.80.

Spot gold rose 0.09% to $3,338.65 an ounce. U.S. gold futures (GCc1) settled almost flat at $3,382.60.

Cryptocurrency markets stabilised after bitcoin touched a record $124,480.82 on Thursday. It was down 0.78% at $117,033.52.

data.title: Supercom Ltd reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Supercom Ltd reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 64 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.20. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 36 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 3 cents to 68 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 5.4% to $7.14 million from a year ago; analysts expected $6.46 million.

  • Supercom Ltd's reported EPS for the quarter was 64 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $1.1 million.

  • Supercom Ltd shares had fallen by 6.3% this quarter and gained 101.8% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the software peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Supercom Ltd is $15.00, about 35.2% above its last closing price of $9.73

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data August 15 at 08:58 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: RioCan Real Estate Investment Trust Announces August 2025 Distribution
data.description:
TORONTO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--August 15, 2025--

RioCan Real Estate Investment Trust ("RioCan") today announced a distribution of 9.65 cents per unit for the month of August. The distribution will be payable on September 8, 2025, to unitholders of record as at August 29, 2025.

About RioCan

RioCan meets the everyday shopping needs of Canadians through the ownership, management and development of necessity-based and mixed-use properties in densely populated communities. As at June 30, 2025, our portfolio is comprised of 178 properties with an aggregate net leasable area of approximately 32 million square feet (at RioCan's interest). To learn more about us, please visit www.riocan.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250815276094/en/

CONTACT: RioCan Real Estate Investment Trust

Investor Relations Inquiries

Email: ir@riocan.com

data.title: Minto Apartment REIT Announces August 2025 Cash Distribution

Payment will be made on September 15, 2025 to unitholders of record as at August 31, 2025.

About Minto Apartment Real Estate Investment Trust

Minto Apartment Real Estate Investment Trust is an unincorporated, open-ended real estate investment trust established pursuant to a declaration of trust under the laws of the Province of Ontario to own income-producing multi-residential properties located in urban markets in Canada. The REIT owns a portfolio of high-quality income-producing multi-residential rental properties located in Toronto, Montreal, Ottawa, Calgary and Vancouver. data.title: Killam Apartment REIT Announces August 2025 Distribution
data.description:
HALIFAX, NS, Aug. 15, 2025 /CNW/ - Killam Apartment REIT is pleased to announce its August 2025 monthly distribution. The distribution of $0.06 per unit will be paid on September 15, 2025, to unitholders of record on August 29, 2025.

Killam Apartment REIT offers a distribution reinvestment plan (the "DRIP"). Eligible unitholders may reinvest their cash distributions, on each distribution payment date, in additional units. Participating unitholders will receive an additional distribution of units representing 3% of the amount of the distribution reinvested pursuant to the DRIP.

About Killam Apartment REIT

Killam Apartment REIT, based in Halifax, Nova Scotia, is one of Canada's largest residential real estate investment trusts, owning, operating, managing and developing a $5.5 billion portfolio of apartments and manufactured home communities. Killam's strategy to enhance value and profitability focuses on three priorities: 1) increasing earnings from existing operations, 2) expanding the portfolio and diversifying geographically through accretive acquisitions which target newer properties and through the disposition of non-core assets, and 3) developing high-quality properties in its core markets.

For information, please contact:

Claire Hawksworth, CPA

Senior Manager, Investor Relations

chawksworth@killamreit.com

(902) 442-5322

Note: The Toronto Stock Exchange has neither approved nor disapproved of the information contained herein.

SOURCE Killam Apartment Real Estate Investment Trust

data.title: FIRST NATIONAL FINANCIAL CORPORATION ANNOUNCES AUGUST DIVIDEND PAYMENT
data.description:
TORONTO, Aug. 15, 2025 /CNW/ - First National Financial Corporation today announced its monthly dividend payment of $0.208334 per common share for the period August 1 to August 31, 2025. The dividend will be payable on September 15, 2025, to shareholders of record at the close of business on August 29, 2025.

First National, for the purposes of the Income Tax Act (Canada) and any similar provincial legislation, advises that its dividends declared in August 2025 will be eligible dividends, unless otherwise indicated.

About First National Financial Corporation

First National Financial Corporation (, , ) is the parent company of First National Financial LP, a Canadian-based originator, underwriter and servicer of predominantly prime residential (single-family and multi-unit) and commercial mortgages. With almost $160 billion in mortgages under administration, First National is one of Canada's largest non-bank originator and underwriter of mortgages and is among the top three in market share in the mortgage broker distribution channel. For more information, please visit www.firstnational.ca.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 PM ET: Retail Sales Notch Another Strong Month. Wall Street Was Depending on It. | Americans ...
data.description:
Retail Sales Notch Another Strong Month. Wall Street Was Depending on It.

Consumer spending ticked up in July in line with economists' expectations, suggesting American households remain resilient.

----

Americans more worried about inflation and unemployment after new trade deals leave tariffs at highest level in decades

Fresh worries about inflation soured Americans on the economy in early August, underscoring lingering anxiety about the highest U.S. tariffs in decades and a further rise in unemployment.

----

Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: Fed's Powell Comments at Jackson Hole in Focus

Comments from U.S. Federal Reserve President Jerome Powell at the Jackson Hole Symposium, alongside minutes to the Fed's most recent meeting, will be closely watched as investors increasingly anticipate that interest-rate cuts will resume next month.

----

U.S. Stocks End Mixed, Dow Boosted by UnitedHealth

U.S. stocks were ended mixed, with the Dow edging higher after a vote of confidence from Warren Buffett in struggling health insurer UnitedHealth.

----

Trump, Putin Head to High-Stakes Alaska Summit With Clashing Objectives

The two leaders are set to meet in person for the first time since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

----

Why Haven't Tariffs Boosted Inflation? This Theory Is Gaining Traction

New research suggests the actual tariff rates are well below what economists have suspected.

----

China's Economy Shows Signs of Slowing, Raising Pressure on Beijing

The Chinese economy lost some momentum in July, backing the case for policymakers to snap out of wait-and-see mode and stimulate growth.

----

Japan's Economy Records Modest Growth Despite Trade Uncertainty

Japan's latest economic data has calmed recession fears, showing a fifth straight quarter of growth despite the impact of U.S. tariffs.

----

Swiss Economy Slows Sharply as Tariffs Weigh

Switzerland's economic growth slowed sharply in the second quarter as U.S. tariff frontrunning seen earlier in the year unwound.

----

M&A is back and corporate earnings are up. Do America's biggest companies need rate cuts?

America's largest companies aren't making a slam-dunk case for the Federal Reserve to quickly slash interest rates.

----

Canada PM Carney Aims to Resolve Canola Row With China

Officials will work on a support package for canola growers while trying to resolve the trade row with their Chinese counterparts over the oilseed, said Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney.

data.title: CopAur Minerals Sells Bolo Project Interest, Strengthening Focus on Flagship Kinsley Mountain
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - August 15, 2025) - CopAur Minerals Inc.  (the "Company" or "CopAur") announces that it has sold its minority interest in the Bolo Gold Project (the "Property") to Allegiant Gold Ltd. ("Allegiant").

This transaction is consistent with the Company's strategic decision to focus its attention and resources on advancing its 100%-owned, past-producing Kinsley Mountain Oxide Gold Project in Nevada.

Under the terms of the agreement, CopAur will receive cash consideration and the forgiveness of certain debt obligations owed to Allegiant by CopAur.

About CopAur

CopAur is an exploration company focused on developing projects within the emerging, mineral-rich mining regions of Nevada. The Company is backed by a dynamic and experienced team of resource professionals advancing its projects in Nevada with the flagship project being Kinsley Mountain Gold Project, a Carlin-style project located 90 kilometres south of the Long Canyon mine (currently in production under the Newmont/Barrick joint venture, Nevada Gold Mines).

On Behalf Of The Board Of CopAur Minerals Inc.Conrad Swanson, Chairman

For more information, please contact ir@copaur.com

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release.

data.title: Moatable, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Moatable, Inc., a leading provider of SaaS solutions, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing significant improvements in both financial and operational performance. The company's diverse portfolio, including its flagship platforms Lofty and Trucker Path, has driven substantial growth and positioned Moatable, Inc. for continued success in the SaaS industry.

Financial Highlights

Moatable, Inc. reported robust financial results for the latest quarter, reflecting the company's strategic focus on expanding its SaaS offerings and improving operational efficiency. Key financial metrics include:

  • Total Revenues: $19.276 million, reflecting an increase from the previous period driven by growth in SaaS revenue.
  • Gross Profit: $14.293 million, indicating improved efficiency in cost management.
  • (Loss) Income from operations: $0.446 million, showing a positive turnaround from a loss in the previous period.
  • Net (loss) income: $0.435 million, demonstrating a recovery from a net loss in the prior period.
  • Net (loss) income per share attributable to Moatable, Inc. shareholders - Basic: $0.0006, highlighting a return to profitability on a per-share basis.
  • Net (loss) income per share attributable to Moatable, Inc. shareholders - Diluted: $0.0006, consistent with basic earnings per share, reflecting no dilution effect.

Business Highlights

Moatable, Inc. continues to make significant strides in its operational performance, driven by its innovative SaaS platforms and strategic acquisitions. Key business highlights include:

  • Revenue Segments: Moatable, Inc. operates two main SaaS platforms, Lofty and Trucker Path. Lofty focuses on real estate sales acceleration and client lifecycle management, while Trucker Path provides a driver-centric online transportation management platform. For the three months ended June 30, 2025, Lofty generated $9,895 in revenue, and Trucker Path generated $9,342.
  • Geographical Performance: The majority of Moatable, Inc.'s revenue is generated from the U.S. market, which remains the primary focus for its SaaS businesses. The company continues to leverage its strong presence in the U.S. to drive growth in its core segments.
  • New Product Launches: During the reporting period, Moatable, Inc. acquired Truckers Best Insurance LLC to enter the insurance brokerage business, expanding its service offerings within the Trucker Path segment.
  • Future Outlook: Moatable, Inc. is focused on leveraging synergies from its recent acquisition of Truckers Best Insurance LLC to enhance its service offerings and drive future revenue growth. The company is also committed to expanding its SaaS platforms and exploring new market opportunities to sustain its growth trajectory.

SEC Filing: Moatable, Inc. [ MTBLY ] - 10-Q - Aug. 15, 2025

data.title: GRAPHJET TECHNOLOGY SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Graphjet Technology, a company specializing in the production of high-quality graphene and graphite, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2024. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business activities during the reporting period. Despite not generating revenue, Graphjet has made significant strides in its business operations, including a notable merger and advancements in production technology.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $0 million. The company did not generate any revenue during the reporting period.
  • Gross Profit: $0 million. With no revenue, the gross profit remains zero.
  • Loss from operations: $(1.21) million. Reflects a decrease in operating expenses compared to the previous year.
  • Net loss: $(1.27) million. The company continues to incur losses as it has not yet commenced sales of its products.
  • Loss per share ordinary share - Basic: $(0.01). Reflects the net loss distributed over the weighted average number of shares.

Business Highlights

  • Business Combination: On March 14, 2024, Graphjet Technology completed a merger with Energem, resulting in Graphjet becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary of Energem. This merger was accounted for as a reverse recapitalization, with Graphjet treated as the acquirer for financial reporting purposes.
  • Organization and Nature of Business: Graphjet Technology is focused on the production of high-quality graphene and graphite, which are critical raw materials used in various industries such as energy storage, electronics, aerospace, and advanced manufacturing.
  • Production Technology: The company utilizes a patented technology that transforms palm kernel shells, a sustainable and renewable agricultural waste product, into artificial graphene and graphite. This process is noted for its lower carbon emissions compared to traditional methods.
  • Research and Development: Graphjet collaborates with the National University of Malaysia and University Teknikal Malaysia Melaka for research and development in graphite and graphene applications, with the Massachusetts Institute of Technology as part of its Industrial Liaison Program.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to sample its graphite and graphene products to multinational companies for market acceptance and procurement purposes, aiming to replace current high-cost suppliers. Starting from June 2025, Graphjet has begun generating revenue from selling side products.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are primarily based in Malaysia, and its functional currency is the Malaysian Ringgit. The company is exposed to foreign exchange risk due to fluctuations between the USD and RM.
  • Segment Reporting: The company operates as a single segment, focusing on the production and development of graphene and graphite products.
  • Lease Commitments: Graphjet has entered into operating lease agreements for its factory in Selangor, Malaysia, with lease terms extending through January 2026.
  • New Equipment Acquisition: On June 26, 2025, Graphjet purchased new equipment valued at approximately $299,000 to enhance production capacity and improve the quality of graphite produced.

SEC Filing: GRAPHJET TECHNOLOGY [ GTI ] - 10-Q - Aug. 15, 2025

data.title: CVS Health Sells About $4 Billion of Notes
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

CVS Health has agreed to sell and issue about $4 billion in notes.

The healthcare giant entered into an underwriting agreement with Barclays, J.P. Morgan Securities and Wells Fargo Securities on Aug. 11, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday.

Under the agreement, the company said it would sell $750 million in 5% senior notes due in 2032, $1.5 billion in 5.5% senior notes due in 2035, $1.25 billion in 6.2% senior notes due in 2055 and $500 million in 6.3% senior notes due in 2065.

The notes were issued Aug. 15, the company said.

The company expects net proceeds of about $3.96 billion, after deducting the underwriters' discount and estimated offering expenses.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Integrity Data Solutions Completes Acquisition of iDoc Corp
data.description:
PHOENIX--(BUSINESS WIRE)--August 15, 2025--

Integrity Data Solutions ("ID Solutions" or the "Company"), through its subsidiary Compu-Call Holdings, LLC ("Compu-Call"), announced that it has acquired iDoc Corp ("iDoc"). The acquisition enhances ID Solutions' ability to deliver a complete range of copy and print solutions, with expanded hardware options, short- and long-term rentals, leasing programs and on-site support.

iDoc is a trusted provider of commercial copier and print hardware, leasing, rental and service throughout Texas. These capabilities will be combined with ID Solutions' comprehensive barcoding and print hardware offerings, managed print services and hardware service solutions, creating a full spectrum offering for customers.

"This acquisition strengthens our position in the print solutions market and expands our hardware and service offerings," said Merrill Matthews, CEO of ID Solutions. "With iDoc's experience and customer base, we can now deliver even more value — whether that's leasing or selling a printer or copier, setting up a rental fleet for a seasonal project, or keeping equipment running at peak performance through our service programs."

"We are excited to become part of ID Solutions," said Bob Buban, Co-Founder and CEO of iDoc Corp. "Our combined resources and expertise mean customers can expect a broader product selection, more flexible and comprehensive solutions, and the same outstanding service they've come to expect."

Integrity Data Solutions is a portfolio company of Falx Capital LLC, a Denver-based private investment company. iDoc represents the third acquisition by ID Solutions since Falx's acquisition of the Company in 2023.

About iDoc Corp:

Founded 1995, iDoc Corp provides commercial copier and printer hardware sales, leasing, rentals, and service, delivering reliable equipment and responsive service to businesses throughout Texas. Learn more at www.idoccorp.com.

About ID Solutions:

ID Solutions is a leading provider of automated data collection, print and supply chain solutions. With a comprehensive portfolio of barcoding and print hardware, maintenance and support services, managed print solutions, and asset tracking software, ID Solutions serves as a trusted partner to customers across North America. Learn more at www.accessids.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250815117038/en/

CONTACT: Merrill Matthews

mmatthews@falxcapital.com

data.title: Dow ends higher after UnitedHealth gains, other indexes slip on rate cut uncertainty
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indexes: Dow up 0.08%, S&P 500 down 0.29%, Nasdaq off 0.4%
  • UnitedHealth surges after Berkshire Hathaway investment
  • Bank of America shares down on Berkshire share sale
  • Applied Materials drops on weak China demand forecast
  • Investors monitor Trump-Putin summit at Alaska

The blue-chip Dow Jones ended higher after hitting an intraday record high on Friday, as UnitedHealth's shares jumped after Berkshire Hathaway raised its stake, but other Wall Street indexes slipped as mixed data clouded the Federal Reserve's next monetary policy move.

A meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin was also on the radar, with markets hoping it could pave the way for a resolution to the Ukraine conflict and determine the outlook for crude prices. The two leaders began a meeting in Alaska on Friday afternoon.

UnitedHealth Group rose almost 12%, its biggest single-day percentage rise since March 2020, after Warren Buffett's company revealed a new investment in the health insurer, while Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management also turned more bullish on the company.

Rising costs in the broader healthcare sector and about a 40% slump in UnitedHealth's shares this year have left the Dow lagging its Wall Street peers on the road to record highs. The price-weighted index last scaled an all-time high on December 4.

The healthcare sector gained 1.65% on Friday, tracking its best weekly performance since October 2022.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 34.86 points, or 0.08%, to 44,946.12, the S&P 500 lost 18.74 points, or 0.29%, to 6,449.80 and the Nasdaq Composite lost 87.69 points, or 0.40%, to 21,622.98.

More broadly, Wall Street's main stock indexes recorded their second week of gains, buoyed by expectations that the Fed could restart its monetary policy easing cycle with a 25-basis-point interest rate cut in September.

The central bank last lowered borrowing costs in December and said U.S. tariffs could add to price pressures. However, recent labor market weakness and signs that tariff-induced inflation was yet to reflect in headline consumer prices have made investors confident of a potential dovish move next month.

"The question is, has the tariff gotten into the price of goods yet? And it appears that there hasn't," said Joe Saluzzi, co-head of equity trading at Themis Trading.

Saluzzi also said while markets have largely priced in a September rate cut, investors might be overlooking risks, with low volatility and rich valuations pointing to a sense of complacency.

In a mixed day for economic data, a report showed retail sales in July rose as expected, but consumer confidence and factory production numbers indicated tariffs were taking a toll on other pockets of the economy.

"Today is a normal pullback given the recent strong gains," said Eric Teal, chief investment officer at Comerica Wealth Management.

"We will have to wait for the personal consumption expenditures data which the Fed prefers and has been running a bit hotter than CPI of late to see how much inflation is finding its way into the system."

Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee was also cautionary in his remarks.

Trump has said he will unveil tariffs on steel and semiconductors next week.

Among other stocks that were on the move, Applied Materials tumbled 14% after the chip equipment maker issued weak fourth-quarter forecasts.

Shares of Bank of America dropped 1.6% after Berkshire Hathaway reduced its stake in the second-biggest U.S. lender by 4.2% to 605.3 million shares. It still owns about an 8% stake in BofA.

Intel rose 2.9% after a report said the Trump administration was in talks for the U.S. government to potentially take a stake in the chipmaker.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.3-to-1 ratio on the NYSE. There were 244 new highs and 45 new lows on the NYSE.

On the Nasdaq, declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.36-to-1 ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 10 new 52-week highs and no new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 79 new highs and 81 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was relatively light, with 16.3 billion shares traded, compared to an average of 18.2 billion shares over the previous 20 sessions.

For the week, the S&P 500 gained 0.94%, the Nasdaq rose 0.81%, and the Dow climbed 1.74%. The Russell 2000 Small Cap Index rose 3.13%.

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1436 ET - The market for fragrance dupes now smells a little sweeter. Luxury-perfume lookalikes are rapidly trending on TikTok Shop, with Oakcha leading the surge, Jefferies analysts wrote in a note. The company saw revenue growth in the first half of the year, driven by affordable alternatives to other luxury scents. The popularity of fragrance dupes has driven excitement for TikTok Shop, which analysts say is evolving into a major beauty marketplace and is becoming a strong competitor to other channels. TikTok Shop, as well as Amazon.com, is drawing share from additional channels as the online retailers continue to lean into niche strategies, the analysts said. (natalie.weger@wsj.com; @NatalieWeger)

1402 ET - Spotify's recent price increases in some countries demonstrate a strategy where the company invests in a new content vertical, drives engagement and then raises prices, Deutsche Bank analysts say. The streaming giant's wave of price step-ups in Germany, Austria, Australia and Liechtenstein are higher than expected, and appear to be related to its audiobooks and possibly its video podcasts, the analysts say. The increases should benefit margins given the higher flow-through of non-music content, and could drive about 50 million euros in incremental revenue in 4Q, and another 350 million euros in revenue in 2026, they estimate.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1147 ET - Palo Alto Networks' next quarterly results should show continuing strength when it comes to cyber deal activity as its platformization strategy gains further traction heading into FY26, analysts at Wedbush say in a note. The analysts say the cybersecurity company is one of their favorite names to own for the next 18 months as it generates a more stable pipeline, while also seeing incremental strength in deal flow in the past few months. "Despite the recent oversold drawdown in Palo's stock tied to the CyberArk Software acquisition, we view this as a golden buying opportunity as the company completes its platform approach and provides a one-stop shop for cybersecurity," the analysts say. Shares up less than 1% at $175.14. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1113 ET - As next-gen AI models expand into areas beyond data centers, demand is growing for Sandisk, Mizuho analysts wrote in a note. The analysts see Sandisk as a leading player in the NAND flash market, with strong position in consumer, client and cloud markets. The company reported revenue and earnings per share above expectations in its most recent quarter, but also released weaker third-quarter guidance. Shares dropped 10% after hours Thursday, but analysts said the reaction was overdone as the company continues to see momentum in the NAND flash market. (natalie.weger@wsj.com; @NatalieWeger)11:08 — Applied Materials' weak fourth-quarter outlook is a sign that the company may be losing share in the semiconductor industry, says Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya, who downgrades the stock to neutral. The company said it was seeing uneven demand from customers as a result of economic uncertainty, but Arya says the slowdown may be specific to Applied Materials. Some of its competitors are taking share of the wafer fabrication equipment business, particularly in foundry/logic, while Applied Materials may have high exposure to sluggish product categories, such as over-supplied mature node semiconductors. Shares are down 12% to $164.73. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1033 ET - WELL Health Technologies' bet on Canada seems to be paying off after a strong 2Q. CIBC's Scott Fletcher and Erin Kyle say in a report that 2Q results were solid, with revenue modestly ahead of consensus and adjusted Ebitda 3% above views. They note that WELL continues to focus its efforts on growing the Canadian business, acquiring three clinics in the quarter and an additional four clinics post quarter-end, adding C$23 million in yearly revenue. "Performance in the Canadian business was strong in the quarter, with revenue from Canadian patient services and SaaS and Technology up 40% Y/Y and 25% organically," they say. In the meantime, asset sales discussions continue and WELL remains committed to exit the patient care and digital delivery businesses in the U.S., the analysts say. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0941 ET - Wing Stop's "Smart Kitchen" technology is a game changer for the business, says Raymond James analyst Brian Vaccaro, who upgrades the stock to strong buy from outperform. The system, which replaced the company's paper ticket system and uses AI-driven demand forecasting to allow kitchens to prepare for surges, is showing very encouraging results. At locations with the technology, ticket times have fallen by about half, customer satisfaction is higher and delivery times have dropped below 30 minutes. Those improvements plus a new loyalty program in 2026 and a committed franchise development pipeline at all-time highs should drive high teens-to-low 20s annual EBITDA growth over the next few years, Vaccaro says. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0902 ET - D.A. Davidson says Salesforce's challenges are appropriately priced in for a business seeing organic growth decelerate in its core business and mounting competitive pressure. That's prompting the investment bank to upgrade shares to neutral from underperform. Analysts Gil Luria and Clark Wright also say in a research note that activist investor Starboard's increased stake in the company may pressure management to refocus growth on the core business. The analysts maintain the $225 target price. Salesforce is up 1% to $236.50 premarket. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0852 ET - The most interesting detail of the latest 13F filing from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is that the Norges Bank, Norway's central bank, increased its exposure to bitcoin in the second quarter, Standard Chartered's Geoff Kendrick says in a note. Norges Bank bought shares in bitcoin treasury companies Strategy, formerly known as Microstrategy, and Metaplanet, he says. "In the second quarter, they increased their bitcoin equivalent exposure from 6,200 to 11,400 bitcoins, an 83% increase." This position remains almost entirely in Strategy, although Norges did add a small bitcoin equivalent position in Metaplanet. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0440 ET - Tencent has strong growth potential across its business units, boosted by AI technology over the next three to five years, CGS International analysts say in a note. They expect Tencent's 3Q gross profit margin and adjusted operating profit margin to rise to 56.4% and 38.2%, respectively, after the company's better-than-expected 2Q profit. They also project continued revenue growth and lift their 2025-2027 EPS forecasts by 1.4%-3.1%. However, AI-related capital expenditure is set to increase in 2H compared with 1H after Nvidia received U.S. approval to resume H20 AI chip sales to China. The brokerage raises its target price to HK$693.00 from HK$654.00 and maintains an add rating. Shares close 0.3% higher at HK$592.00. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

0049 ET - Lenovo Group is well-positioned to capture growth opportunities in AI infrastructure and edge AI, Citi analysts say in a research note. The company's operational flexibility in product design, manufacturing and supply-chain management should help it navigate macro volatility, they say. Lenovo's shares fell Thursday despite a fiscal 1Q profit beat, as gross profit margin weakness weighed and profit-taking likely emerged after a recent rally. However, Lenovo's stocks remain attractive to Citi due to its solid fundamentals and attractive valuation. Citi maintains its buy rating on Lenovo and raises its target price to HK$13.60 from HK$13.30 given its solid outlook. Shares are last at HK$10.65. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0004 ET - NetEase's game gross receipts are likely to improve sequentially in 3Q on major game updates, Daiwa analysts say in a research note. Major updates of legacy titles such as "Once Human" and "Identity V" happened in July, with strong momentum heading into 3Q, they say. Given the low base in 2H 2024 due to the adjustments for "Fantasy Westward Journey" and "Eggy Party," Daiwa expects revenue growth in online games to accelerate to 16.5% on year in 3Q. Meanwhile, the recently launched "Marvel Mystic Mayhem" still has a chance to improve its gross receipts, as NetEase plans content updates every six weeks to maintain its one million player base, they add. Daiwa raises its target price on the Chinese videogame company to HK$260.00 from HK$235.00 while keeping a buy rating. Shares are last at HK$204.80. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1445 ET - Eli Lilly says it agrees with President Trump's push for lower drug prices in the U.S., but BofA analysts think the GLP-1 company is unlikely to cut list prices significantly so it can avoid hurting its own finances.Instead, Lilly is promoting a direct-to-consumer sales model called Lilly Direct that bypasses third-parties and aims to offer lower net prices. Lilly Direct is more of a public relations move and is unlikely to meaningfully lower drug costs on obesity treatments, the analysts say. Lilly also plans to raise prices in the UK and possibly other European countries. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1033 ET - WELL Health Technologies' bet on Canada seems to be paying off after a strong 2Q. CIBC's Scott Fletcher and Erin Kyle say in a report that 2Q results were solid, with revenue modestly ahead of consensus and adjusted Ebitda 3% above views. They note that WELL continues to focus its efforts on growing the Canadian business, acquiring three clinics in the quarter and an additional four clinics post quarter-end, adding C$23 million in yearly revenue. "Performance in the Canadian business was strong in the quarter, with revenue from Canadian patient services and SaaS and Technology up 40% Y/Y and 25% organically," they say. In the meantime, asset sales discussions continue and WELL remains committed to exit the patient care and digital delivery businesses in the U.S., the analysts say. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1849 ET [Dow Jones]--The volume growth assumed by Cochlear's profit guidance looks conservative to its bulls at Jefferies. Analysts David Stanton and Vanessa Thomson tell clients in a note that the hearing-implant provider's guidance for a fiscal 2026 underlying net profit of between A$435 million and A$460 million is slightly short of consensus for A$461 million. However, they point out that the assumption of 10% volume growth looks conservative given the likely scale of the impact from the release of a new model. Jefferies has a last published buy rating and A$354.00 target price on the stock, which is at A$305.99 ahead of the open. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1806 ET - Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management reports several new holdings including Lululemon in its quarterly 13F filing and has no put options, in contrast to the firms's previous filing in May. "The Big Short" investor took new positions UnitedHealth Group and Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, which include stock and call options, are each valued at more than $100 million. Scion reports a new Bruker stake after exiting from the stock during the first quarter. Meanwhile, Scion reports call options for Alibaba Group Holding and JD.com, a quarter after it reported put options for both. (josh.beckerman@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1512 ET - Crude futures return most of the previous day's gains as traders remain cautious ahead of the meeting between Presidents Trump and Putin about ending the Russia-Ukraine war. "Crude continues trading in a defensive pattern," BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler says in a note. Most traders view the meeting as preliminary, he says, "with no real results coming forth today which means Russian petroleum exports will continue." While a cease-fire announcement would be a near-term negative for oil, the possibility of Trump threatening more tariff pressure against buyers of Russian oil in the event of a stalemate "is keeping a nervous trade to crude." WTI settles down 1.8% at $62.80 a barrel for a 1.7% loss on the week. Brent falls 1.5% to $65.85 a barrel, down 1.1% on the week. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1333 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. edged up for a second consecutive week, with operators adding one rig to 412, Baker Hughes reports. That's 71 fewer than a year earlier. Despite the declining rig count, oil production reached a record 13.49 million barrels a day in May, according to the EIA. The agency said this week that it expects U.S. production to hit a record near 13.6 million b/d in December, before falling to 13.1 million b/d by 4Q26 as oil prices fall and producers accelerate the decrease in drilling and well completions. The latest EIA output estimates put average crude oil production at 13.3 million b/d for the four weeks through Aug. 8. Rigs directed at natural gas slipped by one this week to 122, or 24 more than a year ago. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0945 ET - Oil futures give back a chunk of yesterday's gains as the market awaits the outcome of the summit meeting in Alaska between Presidents Trump and Putin. While Trump has warned of "severe consequences" if Putin won't agree to a cease-fire in the Russia-Ukraine war, he also expressed optimism that something will come of the meeting. While recent pricing points to the oil market's "very relaxed view of ongoing geopolitical stories," lack of agreement could put upward pressure on prices, Neil Crosby of Sparta Commodities says in a note. "No deal might well mean more pressure exerted on Russia and its buyers, too, which may send India even deeper into the spot crude market and even see its government mandate all refiners to wean off Russian." WTI is down 1.2% at $63.17 a barrel, and Brent is off 1.1% at $66.12. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

2219 ET - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index may well gain another 4% to end 2025 at 1640, possibly driven by laggard heavyweights like banks and Tenaga Nasional, Hong Leong Investment Bank analyst Chan Jit Hoong says in a note. Foreign investors, currently holding a record low 19% of the Bursa Malaysia market, have largely stayed out of the recent rally, he says, adding that their return could provide a significant boost to the index. He expects two U.S. rate cuts this year, which may support ringgit and attract inflows. Amid a challenging macro backdrop, Chan favors a strategy of buying stocks with higher volatility on dips, alongside resilient big-cap stocks and domestic growth names. The KLCI is down 0.3% at 1575.69. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2112 ET - Ampol's A$1.1 billion deal to buy gas-station owner EG Australia pushes out the prospect of special dividends, but the trade-off seems sensible to Macquarie. Ampol will largely fund the transaction using A$800 million of cash and the issue of A$250 million of equity to the vendor. It means Ampol will transition from supplying fuels to the EG gas stations, to running the sites. "The EG opportunity, coupled with U-GO strategy, places Ampol back on a structural earnings growth trajectory (in non-refining)," Macquarie says. U-GO is Ampol's experiment with unmanned gas stations. Macquarie retains a neutral call on Ampol. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2105 ET - For Origin Energy, FY 2026 could be the peak year for capital expenditure. That's according to Macquarie, which highlights an emerging theme that Origin is becoming capital light. It says Origin is likely to continue to invest in batteries, but this is "likely to be off balance sheet." Origin intends to bring in a capital partner to fund most of the capex growth required to build the Yanco wind farm. Macquarie also notes that capex at Origin's Eraring coal-fired power plant has likely peaked, and should moderate as it heads toward closure in FY 2028. Macquarie retains a neutral call on Origin. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1843 ET - Jefferies draws parallels to Ampol's acquisition of Z Energy when assessing the merits of its latest deal. Ampol said late yesterday it will buy gas-station owner EG Australia for A$1.1 billion. Analyst Michael Simotas recalls that there was some initial skepticism about Ampol's deal for Z Energy in 2021. That deal turned out to be a resounding success, in part due to Ampol's price discipline. "Proposed acquisition of EG Australia offers similarly strong strategic rationale, given substantial synergies, U-GO conversion opportunity, and potential to stem the volume drag on Fuels & Infrastructure," Jefferies says. U-GO is Ampol's experiment with unmanned gas stations, and Jefferies says Ampol is paying a sensible price to acquire EG Australia. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0333 ET - Capital expenditure momentum in the mining industry remains solid with growth expectations of 5% for 2025, a small uptick from the 4% previously expected, RBC Capital Markets analysts say. Capex for copper and gold, the most relevant commodities for original equipment makers, should grow faster at 10% and 11% respectively, from a previous 9% estimate for both, RBC says. Still, much of the recent increase ratio can be attributed to inflation and implies little investment beyond what is required to sustain current output, RBC says. "Our view therefore remains that mining capex can continue to grow mid-single digit to high single digit on average for the remainder of the decade," RBC says. Antofagasta shares are up 3.5%, with Glencore up 3% and Rio Tinto up 1.75%. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0230 ET - AIA Engineering's demand outlook for its products appears uncertain, Nomura's Umesh Raut says in a research report. Management didn't share FY 2025 earnings guidance given uncertain demand, the analyst notes. The Indian company's U.S. exports are currently subjected to antidumping duty of roughly 10% and Section 232 tariffs of 50%, the analyst says. The high tariffs have resulted in uncertainties in the U.S. and have substantially dented the company's visibility for U.S. demand beyond the near-term. Nomura cuts its FY 2026-2027 revenue forecasts for AIA Engineering by 6%. It lowers the stock's target price to INR3,078.00 from INR3,140.00, with an unchanged reduce rating. Shares last closed at INR3,114.90. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2321 ET - Iron ore is lower in early Asian trading. Investors' sentiment has cooled on tighter market regulation, leading to sharp declines in prices of coking coal, according to Nanhua Futures analysts. That in turn dragged down iron-ore prices, they say. Overall, prices are likely to stay rangebound in the near term, as iron ore's fundamentals remain stable, they write in a commentary. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange falls 1.4% to CNY774.0 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

2220 ET - Aluminum trades higher in the Asian session, with the three-month LME contract adding 0.2% to $2624.00 a ton. China's demand for aluminum appears to be picking up, compared with a year earlier. Citi says its calculations made with inventory data from Chinese consultant Mysteel show China's year-to-date overall aluminum apparent consumption is likely 4.9% higher on year. Apparent consumption of aluminum ingots and billets over the same period are also 4.2% and 8.0% higher, respectively, Citi estimates. Citi views ingot and billet inventory data as more representative for calculating overall aluminum demand. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1410 ET - AutoCanada underpeformed in 2Q, but its cost reduction plan is picking up speed. CIBC notes in a report that AutoCanada's topline missed market expectations with same-store sales declining by about 3%, when new vehicle sales are up nearly 8% in Canada. However, the company is making progress on its cost-savings program "by not only achieving C$80 million in annualized run-rate cost savings, but by also identifying an additional C$15 million in savings," analysts say. They add that this early progress has provided AutoCanada with greater visibility into the potential of the initiatives, and the company has now identified a credible path to C$115 million in savings. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0836 ET - Hapag-Lloyd's costs were higher-than-expected in the second-quarter and investors might want to see progress on its efficiency program before factoring in the full $1 billion of targeted savings, UBS analysts Cristian Nedelcu and Jonathan Antor write. The weaker-than-expected execution on costs reflects expenses related to its network collaboration with Maersk, port congestion and lower vessel utilization, among other things. UBS lowers its 2025 Ebitda forecasts by 9%, 2026 by 6% and 2027 by 6%, mainly to reflect the higher costs and slightly lower freight rates partially offset by higher volumes. It lowers the stock's price target to 100 euros from 110 euros and keeps a sell rating. Shares fall 0.4% to 121.40 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0313 ET - International Container Terminal Services' volume growth is poised to stay firm in 2H, Maybank Securities' Germaine Guinto says in a research report. Management expects potential upside from exporters shifting to Asia amid tariff uncertainty, the analyst notes. Also, the Philippine container terminal operator is insulated from U.S. trade risks and benefits from a portfolio dominated by gateways with 3X higher yields and greater pricing power than transshipment ports. The brokerage raises its 2025 and 2026 earnings forecasts for the company by 2% and 5%, respectively. It lifts the stock's target price to PHP590.00 from PHP460.00, but keeps a buy rating. Shares are 0.8% higher at PHP485.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2347 ET - Although Pony AI's Gen-7 robotaxi would reach breakeven at the unit level by year-end, it is unlikely to be able to propel the overall robotaxi fleet to a better margin profile until 2026, Daiwa analyst Evelyn Zhang writes in a note. The company has continuously improved its operational efficiency, resulting in a decline in its cost per kilometers to CNY2.5 in 2Q for its Gen-5 and Gen-6 robotaxis. The insurer also lowered the company's insurance premium by 18% on year in 2Q based on its assessment of accident rates, they say. Daiwa maintains buy rating for the stock at target price of $20.00. Shares last closed at $15.15. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

2209 ET - ComfortDelGro's performance may be better in 2H, which tends to be seasonally stronger for the company, OCBC Investment Research's Ada Lim says in a note. The multimodal transport operator is likely to see improvements from ongoing public bus contract renewals at better margins in London, the analyst says. Also, it may get higher revenues from the other public transport segment over the summer holiday season, partly thanks to ongoing active pursuit of tendering opportunities across key markets. OCBC raises the stock's fair value estimate to S$1.75 from S$1.71 to reflect its fine-tuned earnings forecasts for ComfortDelGro, with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.65% higher at S$1.54. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2131 ET - SIA Engineering's shares may fall after failing to clearly break resistance at the S$3.18 level, chart analysis suggests, Phillip Securities Research's Zane Aw says in commentary. The shorter-term 5-day and 10-day simple moving averages also crossed over below the 20-day simple moving average at the start of August, which gives a bearish signal, the analyst notes. The moving average convergence divergence indicator shows decreasing momentum, supporting the bearish view. The share price may pull back and reach S$2.88, a prior horizontal breakout level seen at the beginning of June, the analyst adds. Shares are 0.3% higher at S$3.13. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)
